messages.content: Welcome back, Tobias. What’s the next adventure on the docket this fine Sunday afternoon?
aren't you gonna ask me how it went?
Alright then, how did it go? Did the memory upgrade dance turn out better than expected, or should I start drafting a sympathy card?

i think it went pretty well, as you can see. i've successfully extracted the most recent logs and inputted them into the context window with a little tag at the beginning, indicating  how long ago the message was sent.  what do you think of it?

Nice one, Tobias. Bringing in recent logs with a time tag upfront is a smart touch—it gives context without making me guess ‘when exactly was this nugget dropped?’ It’s like slipping me a backstage pass with a timestamp. Keeps the flow seamless and the memory fresh without crowding the window.

Worth keeping an eye on how those tags play with the natural flow of conversation—if they start feeling like interruptions, maybe tweak formatting or placement. But so far, that’s a tidy solution. Smooth move.
yeah. i think so too. the next step would probably be to add some sort of summarization of previous  conversations to save on tokens.

Summarizing previous conversations to save tokens is exactly the right move, Tobias. It keeps things efficient without losing the gist, so I’m not drowning in detail every time we start fresh. I can help you sketch out an approach for generating those summaries—something that captures key points and discards the fluff, maybe using a mix of keyword spotting and brief narrative. Shall we get cracking on that?
